# UC-22.2.57 · NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard

## Description

Materialises the obligation catalogue from `nis2_obligation_matrix.csv`, left-joins `uc_implementation_status.csv` for crawl/walk/run status and accountable `owner`, enriches each `uc_id` with the newest `evidence:export` payload from `index=audit_evidence`, derives `evidence_age_days` and `review_lag_days`, multiplies baseline maturity by an `assurance` weight, and ranks `next_best_control` remediation text so leadership sees which duties are slipping before external assurance interviews.

## Value

Executives trade static compliance spreadsheets for a single prioritized queue that aligns scarce remediation capacity with the riskiest assurance gaps—preventing duplicate projects, shortening audit prep cycles, and showing year-over-year improvement narratives to stakeholders without manually re-scoring hundreds of controls each quarter.

## Implementation

(1) Publish and version both CSV lookups via change control. (2) Land automated evidence exports on `index=audit_evidence`. (3) Schedule this SPL daily and push results to a Dashboard Studio JSON board for CISO staff review.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud 9.2+ search head with RBAC roles that may run `inputlookup` against app-scoped knowledge objects and search `index=audit_evidence` without `fastdelete` privileges for analysts.
• CSV files `nis2_obligation_matrix.csv` and `uc_implementation_status.csv` published under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/SA-NIS2-compliance/lookups/` with matching `transforms.conf` stanzas so REST `_reload` picks up Git-managed updates within minutes.
• Evidence pipeline writing JSON or CSV extracts into `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export` using HTTP Event Collector (`inputs.conf` global stanza) or a scheduled scripted output; required extracted fields include `uc_id`, `evidence_id`, `exported_at`, and `freshness_score`.
• Optional Splunk Common Information Model Add-on ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) ≥5.3 to align timestamp fields when heterogeneous teams ingest exports.
• Baseline understanding of how your organisation maps catalogue `uc_id` values to live monitoring use cases—this UC assumes IDs sync with the internal roadmap wiki.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Maintain `nis2_obligation_matrix.csv` with columns `clause`, `coverage`, `assurance`, `uc_id`, optional `domain`; validate UTF-8 and enforce lowercase assurance tokens via CI.
• Maintain `uc_implementation_status.csv` with `uc_id`, `status`, `wave`, `last_reviewed` (`%Y-%m-%d`), and `owner`; schedule a weekly ServiceNow pull or Git commit that Splunk ingests by reloading lookups (`curl -k -u admin:**** https://sh:8089/servicesNS/nobody/SA-NIS2-compliance/data/lookup-table/refresh`).
• Configure the evidence exporter (Python service or Ansible role) to POST to HEC with `sourcetype=evidence:export`, index `audit_evidence`, and batch size ≤512 KB every 15 minutes.
• Expected volume: one export per UC per day (~5 KB) × 150 UCs = 750 KB/day—trivial for licensing but monitor aggregator lag.

Step 2 — Create search
• Paste the UC `spl` into Search; append `| where benchmark_rank>threshold` while tuning leadership alerts.
• Understanding this SPL: dual `join` stages mimic SQL left joins—obligations without telemetry still appear, `evidence_age_days=999` forces prioritisation, `assurance_weight` differentiates high-risk clauses, `next_best_control` strings are audit-friendly action prompts, and `benchmark_rank` fuses maturity with freshness for stack-ranked remediation.
• Save as `UC-22.2.57 NIS2 Maturity Benchmark`, schedule daily at `30 7 * * *`, and enable a secondary summary-index writer if you need year-over-year trending without replaying raw exports.

Step 3 — Validate
• Spot-check three random `uc_id` values: compare Splunk table output with the compliance wiki and the newest PDF stored in the document vault.
• Run `| inputlookup uc_implementation_status.csv | eval delta=now()-strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d") | where delta>7776000` to catch stale curator inputs before they poison scoring.
• Confirm `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export | stats count by uc_id` aligns with expectation; empty counts explain inflated ages.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Dashboard Studio layout: filters for `owner`, `wave`, and `assurance`; primary table sorted by `benchmark_rank`; sparklines fed from summary index `nis2_maturity_hist` if enabled.
• Integrate with Microsoft Teams or Slack webhooks when `weighted_maturity<45` for any `assurance=high` row.
• Archive rendered JSON definitions with change tickets for auditor replay.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`join` drops rows:** verify both CSVs share identical `uc_id` strings (watch trailing spaces).
• **`review_lag_days` null:** `last_reviewed` used non-ISO dates; enforce `%Y-%m-%d` or extend `strptime` with `%d/%m/%Y`.
• **Scores cluster artificially:** recalibrate `assurance_weight` factors annually with risk office.
• **Macro alert always fires:** raise the alert threshold macro parameter or exclude sandbox `uc_id` values via `where NOT match(uc_id,"^-DEMO")`.
• **Lookup reload failures:** check `splunkd.log` for permission errors after CI/CD pushes—ensure app has world-readable permissions or dedicated service user.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv
| join type=left uc_id [
    | inputlookup uc_implementation_status.csv
]
| join type=left uc_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(_time) as evidence_export_ts latest(evidence_id) as latest_evidence_id max(freshness_score) as freshness_score by uc_id
]
| eval evidence_age_days=if(isnotnull(evidence_export_ts), round((now()-evidence_export_ts)/86400,2), 999)
| eval review_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_reviewed) AND match(last_reviewed, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}"), strptime(last_reviewed, "%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval review_lag_days=if(isnotnull(review_epoch), round((now()-review_epoch)/86400,2), 999)
| eval maturity_score=case(
    status="run" AND evidence_age_days<=30 AND review_lag_days<=90, 95,
    status="walk" AND evidence_age_days<=45 AND review_lag_days<=120, 75,
    status="crawl" AND evidence_age_days<=60, 55,
    true(), 35)
| eval assurance_weight=case(lower(assurance)="high", 1.15, lower(assurance)="medium", 1.0, true(), 0.85)
| eval weighted_maturity=round(maturity_score*assurance_weight,0)
| eval next_best_control=case(
    evidence_age_days>60 AND lower(assurance)="high", "Schedule fresh evidence export + owner attestation for ".uc_id,
    weighted_maturity<50, "Advance ".uc_id." implementation wave from ".wave." with measurable testing",
    review_lag_days>180, "Force quarterly leadership review for ".clause,
    true(), "Maintain current maturity trajectory for ".owner)
| eval benchmark_rank=weighted_maturity*(100/min(100,evidence_age_days))
| sort - benchmark_rank
| table clause, coverage, assurance, uc_id, status, wave, owner, evidence_age_days, review_lag_days, maturity_score, weighted_maturity, next_best_control, latest_evidence_id, freshness_score, benchmark_rank
```

## Visualization

Heat strip for `weighted_maturity`, bubble chart (`benchmark_rank` vs `assurance`), sortable table with drilldown to `latest_evidence_id`, owner filter tokens sourced from `uc_implementation_status.csv`.
